name: honeycomb
//go-public: DRAFT — see RELEASING.md. To publish: (a) rename to a SCOPED name (the unscoped `honeycomb` is taken on npm), (b) uncomment the publishConfig below, (c) remove `private: true`, (d) set the NPM_TOKEN repo secret. Until then release.yaml's preflight fails closed.
description: Honeycomb monorepo foundation: a long-lived daemon plus thin clients for six coding harnesses, the unified honeycomb CLI, the MCP server, and the embed daemon.
//build-order: Fixed dependency direction enforced by import direction under one tsc pass: (1) core = src/shared + src/daemon-client, (2) connector-base = src/daemon, (3) plugins/native = embeddings, (4) connectors = harnesses/* + mcp + src/cli, (5) assembled distribution = esbuild per-target bundles (PRD-001b). tsc compiles the whole graph in one pass; dependents never import upward, so the single tsc invocation respects this order. PRD-001b appends '&& node esbuild.config.mjs' to the build script; that seam is intentional.
//exports: PRD-019e: @honeycomb/sdk subpath exports. The core entry (.) is the fetch-only, browser-safe HoneycombClient; the three framework helpers ship as separate entry points reusing the core's token/actor model. react + ai are peerDependencies (never bundled into core).

scripts.mutation: npm run mutation:sql && npm run mutation:prune && npm run mutation:normalize && npm run mutation:state
scripts.ci: npm run typecheck && npm run dup && npm run test && npm run audit:sql

//peerDependencies: PRD-019e: react powers @honeycomb/sdk/react; ai powers @honeycomb/sdk/vercel. Both are OPTIONAL peers (peerDependenciesMeta) so the core entry stays dependency-free for Node/Bun/browser. The core client (.) pulls in NEITHER.
//optionalDependencies: PRD-025 D-2: the embedding RUNTIME (@huggingface/transformers + its ONNX native runtime, ~600 MB with the nomic-embed-text-v1.5 model acquired at first-run) ships as an OPTIONAL dependency, NOT in the `files` allowlist. This keeps the published @deeplake/hivemind tarball LEAN (the model is downloaded + cached on the daemon's first warmup, not packed — AC-7) and keeps `npm i` fast for everyone; a `--no-optional` / slimmed / offline install simply runs recall on the BM25/ILIKE lexical fallback (no quality cliff — D-4). The embed daemon dynamic-imports this only at warmup, so the bundle + CI never require it.
